author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, John x. 30, ‘I and the Father are one.’

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John x. 30, ‘I and the Father are one.’

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have heard what the Lord God, Jesus Christ, the Only Son of God, born of God the Father without any mother, and born of a Virgin mother without any human father, said, “I and My Father are One.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Receive ye this, believe it in such wise that ye may attain to understand it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For faith ought to go before understanding, that understanding may be the reward of faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Prophet hath said most expressly, “Unless ye believe, ye shall not understand.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is simply preached is to be believed; what is with exactness discussed, is to be understood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: At first then to imbue your minds with faith we preach to you Christ, the Only Son of God the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why is added, “The Only Son”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because He whose Only Son He is, hath many sons by grace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All the rest then, all saints are sons of God by grace, He Alone by Nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who are sons of God by grace are not What the Father is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And no saint hath ever dared to say, what that Only Son saith, “I and My Father are One.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is He not then our Father too?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If He be not our Father, how say we when we pray, “Our Father, which art in heaven”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But we are sons whom He hath made sons by His Own will, not begotten as sons of His Own Nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in truth He hath begotten us too, but as it is said, as adopted ones, begotten by the favour of His adoption, not by Nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And this too are we called, for that “God hath called us into the adoption of sons;” we are though adopted, men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is called the Only Son, the Only Begotten, in that, He is That which the Father is; but we are men, The Father is God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In then that He is That which the Father is; He said, and said truly, “I and My Father are One.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “are One”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are of one Nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “are One”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are of one Substance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peradventure, ye but imperfectly understand what “of one Substance” is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take we pains that ye may understand it; may God assist both me who speak, and you that hear; me, that I may speak such things as are true and fit for you; and you, that before and above all things ye may believe; and then that ye may understand as best ye can.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is “of One Substance”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let me make use of similitudes to you, that what is imperfectly understood may be made clear by example.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As, suppose, God is gold.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: His Son is gold also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If similitudes ought not to be given for heavenly things from things earthly, how is it written, “Now the Rock was Christ”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, Whatsoever the Father is, This is the Son also; as I have said, for example, “The Father is gold, the Son is gold.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he who says, “The Son is not of the Very Substance which the Father is;” what else says he but, “The Father is gold, the Son is silver”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the Father be gold, and the Son silver; the Only Son hath degenerated from the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A man begets a man; of what substance the father is who begets, of the same substance is the Son who is begotten.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “of the same substance”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The one is a man, and the other is a man; the one hath a soul; so hath the other a soul; the one hath a body, so hath the other a body; what one is, that is the other.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Arian heresy makes answer, and says.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What says it to me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Mark what thou hast said”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What have I said?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That the Son of a man may be compared to the Son of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Certainly he may be compared; but not as you suppose, in strictness of expression; but for a similitude.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But tell me now what you would make of this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Do you not see,” says he, “that the father who begets is greater in age, and the son who is begotten less?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then say ye? tell me; how then say ye, that the Father and the Son, God and Christ, are equal; when ye see that when a man begets a son, the son is less, and the father greater?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wise one, in eternity thou art looking for times; where there are no times, thou art looking for differences of age!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the father is greater in age, and the son less, both are in time; the one groweth, for that the other groweth old.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For by nature, the man, the father, did not beget one less, by nature, as I said, but by age.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wouldest thou know, how that by nature he did not beget one less?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wait, let him grow, and he will be equal to his father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For a little boy even by growing attains to his father’s full size.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whereas you assert that the Son of God is in such wise born less, as never to grow, and by growing even to attain to His Father’s size.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now then a man’s son born of a man, is born in a better condition than the Son of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because the former grows, and attains to his father’s size.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But Christ, if it is as ye say, is in such wise born less, as that He must ever remain less, and no growth of years at least is to be looked for here.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus then you say that there is a diversity in nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why say you so, but because you will not believe the Son to be of the Same Substance which the Father is?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Finally, first acknowledge that He is of the Same Substance, and so call Him less.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider the case of a man, he is a man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is his substance?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is a man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is he whom he begets?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is less, but he is a man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The age is unequal, the nature equal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do you then say too, “What the Father is, That is the Son, but the Son is less”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Say so, make a step forward, say, “of the Same Substance, only less;” and you will get to His being equal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is not a little step you take, it is not a little approach you make to the truth, of acknowledging Him equal, if you shall acknowledge Him to be of the Same Substance, though less.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But He is not of the Same Substance,” this you say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then in that you say this, here is gold and silver; what you say is as if a man were to beget a horse.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For a man is of one substance, a horse of another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then the Son is of another substance than the Father, the Father hath begotten a monster.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when a creature, that is a woman, gives birth to anything that is not a man, it is called a monster.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But that it be not a monster, he that is born is that which he is that begat him, that is, a man and a man, a horse and a horse, a dove and a dove, a sparrow and a sparrow.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To His creatures hath He given to beget that which they are.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To His creatures, to mortal, earthly creatures, hath God given, hath granted to beget that which they are; and thinkest thou that He hath not been able to reserve this for Himself, He who is before all ages?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Should He who hath no beginning of time, beget a son, different from That which Himself is, beget a degenerate son?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear ye how great a blasphemy it is to say, that the Only Son of God is of another substance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Most certainly if He is so, He is degenerate.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If you should say to any child of man, “Thou art degenerate,” how great an offence is it!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet in what sense is any child of man said to be degenerate?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As, for example, his father is brave, he is a poltroon and a coward.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If any one sees him, and would rebuke him, as he thinks of his brave father, what does he say to him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Get thee hence, thou degenerate one!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is “degenerate one”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy father was a brave man, and thou tremblest through fear.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He to whom this is said, is degenerate by some fault, by nature he is equal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “by nature he is equal”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is a man, which his father also is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the one brave, the other a coward; the one bold, the other timid; yet both men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By some fault then he is degenerate, not by nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when you say, that the Only Son, the One Son of the Father, is degenerate, you say nought else, but that He is not What the Father is; and you do not say, that having been already born, He has become degenerate; but He was begotten so.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who can endure this blasphemy?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If they could in any sort whatever see this blasphemy, they would fly from it, and become catholics.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what shall I say, Brethren?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not be angry with them; but pray we for them, that God would give them understanding; for peradventure they were born so.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, were born so?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They receive what they hold from their parents.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They prefer their birth to the truth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them become what they are not, that they may be able to keep what they are; that is, let them become catholics, that they may keep their nature as men; that the creation of God in them perish not, let the grace of God be added to them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they imagine that by their outrage of the Son they honour the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When you say to him, “Thou blasphemest;” he answers, “Why do I blaspheme?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In that thou sayest that the Son is not what the Father is.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he answers me, “Yea, it is thou who blasphemest.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Because thou wouldest make the Son equal to the Father.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I do wish to make the Son equal with the Father, but is this to make a stranger equal?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father rejoiceth when I equal with Him His Only Son; He rejoiceth because He is not envious.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And because God is not envious of His Only Son, therefore did He beget Him Such as He is Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou doest wrong both to the Son, and to the Father Himself, for whose honour thou wouldest do outrage to the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in truth for this reason dost thou say that the Son is not of the Same Substance, lest thou shouldest do wrong to His Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will soon show thee, that thou doest wrong to both.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “How?” saith he.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If I say to any man’s son, Thou art degenerate, thou art not like thy father; degenerate, thou art not what thy father is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The son hears it, and is angry, and says, ‘Was I then born degenerate?’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The father hears it, and is more angry still.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in his anger what says he?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘Have I then begotten a degenerate son?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If I then be one thing, and I have begotten another, I have begotten a monster.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is it then, that whereas thou wishest to pay honour to the One by doing outrage to the Other, thou doest outrage to Both?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou offendest the Son, but thou wilt not propitiate the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When thou honourest the Father by outraging the Son, thou offendest both the Son and the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From whom wilt thou fly? to whom wilt thou fly?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the Father is angry with thee, dost thou fly to the Son?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What doth He say to thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘To whom dost thou fly, to Me, whom thou hast made degenerate?’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the Son is offended, dost thou run to the Father?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He too saith to thee; ‘To whom dost thou fly, to Me who, thou hast said, have begotten a degenerate Son?’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let this suffice for you; hold it fast, commit it to memory, inscribe it in your faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But that ye may understand it, pour out your prayers to God, the Father and the Son, who are One.
